/.title: ggaction
/.description: Most visualization grammars describe a finished chart. ggaction represents chart authoring itself as an immutable, traceable sequence of graphical actions.

/getting-started/.title: Getting Started
/getting-started/.description: This walkthrough installs ggaction, creates a complete scatterplot from an inline dataset, and renders it to Browser Canvas.

/tutorials/.title: Tutorials
/tutorials/.description: Tutorials build complete, runnable charts and explain what each action adds. Choose the visual relationship that matches the question you want to answer.

/tutorials/scatterplot/.title: Scatterplot Tutorial
/tutorials/scatterplot/.description: This tutorial uses the public npm package. The repository also contains a runnable browser example and its canonical program.

/tutorials/polar-points/.title: Polar Point Tutorial
/tutorials/polar-points/.description: This tutorial maps car acceleration to angle, horsepower to distance from the center, and origin to color.

/tutorials/polar-lines/.title: Polar Line and Radar Tutorial
/tutorials/polar-lines/.description: Polar line marks use the same encodeTheta and encodeR actions as Polar points. The line stays open by default; closed: true turns each grouped series into a radar path.

/tutorials/polar-arcs/.title: Polar Arc Tutorial
/tutorials/polar-arcs/.description: Arc marks turn Polar positions into closed sector paths. Use count or weighted sum aggregation for proportional donut sectors, or combine categorical theta bands with a…

/tutorials/line-chart/.title: Line Chart Tutorial
/tutorials/line-chart/.description: This chart shows mean acceleration over time for each origin. The complete module below uses the public npm package.

/tutorials/histogram/.title: Histogram Tutorial
/tutorials/histogram/.description: This chart bins car displacement, counts the rows in each bin, and stacks those counts by origin. The complete module uses the public npm package.

/tutorials/grouped-bar/.title: Bar Chart Tutorial
/tutorials/grouped-bar/.description: This tutorial uses a grouped bar chart as one concrete bar chart layout. It aggregates job percentages by year and places the mean for men and women side by side.

/tutorials/regression-scatterplot/.title: Regression Scatterplot Tutorial
/tutorials/regression-scatterplot/.description: This tutorial filters the cars dataset to Japan and the USA, maps point size to Acceleration and point color/shape to Origin, then adds one linear fit and 95% mean response…

/tutorials/density-area/.title: Density Area Tutorial
/tutorials/density-area/.description: This chart estimates the distribution of car acceleration separately for each Origin.

/tutorials/horizon/.title: Horizon Chart Tutorial
/tutorials/horizon/.description: This chart folds Kenya's life expectancy above and below a 55 year baseline into three compact bands. The source rows stay immutable.

/tutorials/error-bar/.title: Error Bar Tutorial
/tutorials/error-bar/.description: This chart keeps individual car observations visible while summarizing acceleration by Origin.

/tutorials/error-band/.title: Error Band Tutorial
/tutorials/error-band/.description: This chart summarizes life expectancy over time for each Gapminder cluster.

/tutorials/mark-selection/.title: Mark Selection Tutorial
/tutorials/mark-selection/.description: Select final visual items by data field, semantic channel, or concrete graphic property, then filter, reuse, or highlight that selection.

/recipes/.title: Chart Recipes
/recipes/.description: Use a recipe when you know the chart type and want the shortest supported action flow.

/recipes/scatterplot/.title: Scatterplot Recipe
/recipes/scatterplot/.description: Add color: "group", size: "amount", or shape: "category" to the same call for field driven appearance. The default point radius is 3.

/recipes/line-chart/.title: Line Chart Recipe
/recipes/line-chart/.description: For multiple series, add color: "group", groupBy: "group", or strokeDash: { field: "group" } to the facade call.

/recipes/histogram/.title: Histogram Recipe
/recipes/histogram/.description: Add color: { field: "group", layout: "stack" } to the createHistogram options.

/recipes/bar-chart/.title: Bar Chart Recipe
/recipes/bar-chart/.description: The current complete bar chart example uses grouped bars. Grouping is a layout choice inside the bar chart, not a separate top level chart type.

/recipes/heatmap/.title: Heatmap Recipe
/recipes/heatmap/.description: This mode derives rectangular x/y bounds and a count for each cell. It emits the complete grid by default, including zero count cells.

/recipes/regression-scatterplot/.title: Regression Scatterplot Recipe
/recipes/regression-scatterplot/.description: Use this pattern to layer grouped linear fits and mean response confidence bands over a point chart.

/recipes/density-area/.title: Density Area Recipe
/recipes/density-area/.description: Use createGuides({ grid: { horizontal: {}, vertical: {} } }) for both grid directions.

/recipes/horizon/.title: Horizon Chart Recipe
/recipes/horizon/.description: Use editHorizon({ bands, baseline, extent, palette }) to create a revised immutable result. Use resolve: "independent" only when group local amplitude comparison is intentional.

/recipes/error-bar/.title: Error Bar Recipe
/recipes/error-bar/.description: When one compatible layer is already encoded, call createErrorBar() without options to reuse its fields, data, coordinate, and scales.

/recipes/error-band/.title: Error Band Recipe
/recipes/error-band/.description: When one compatible layer is already encoded, omitted data, coordinate, position fields, scales, and explicit grouping are reused when the interval axis is unambiguous.

/recipes/box-plot/.title: Box Plot Recipe
/recipes/box-plot/.description: Use whisker: { type: "minmax" } for observed minimum and maximum whiskers without an outlier layer.

/recipes/violin-plot/.title: Violin Plot Recipe
/recipes/violin-plot/.description: Add color: "category" for category fills. Use split: { field } when the field has exactly two values, or provide an explicit two value domain when side assignment must be stable…

/recipes/composition/.title: Composition Recipe
/recipes/composition/.description: pointRows and barRows are arrays of plain row objects containing the named fields. Every child must already have one complete materialized Canvas.

/recipes/facet/.title: Facet Recipe
/recipes/facet/.description: cars is an array of plain row objects containing the encoded fields and Origin. Facet values retain source first appearance order.

/recipes/parallel-coordinates/.title: Parallel Coordinates Recipe
/recipes/parallel-coordinates/.description: cars is an array of plain row objects. Every eligible row becomes one open path across the ordered dimensions.

/recipes/rose-chart/.title: Rose Chart Recipe
/recipes/rose-chart/.description: A rose chart compares several magnitudes inside equal categorical angle bands. The overlay layout draws larger sectors first so smaller sectors remain visible.

/recipes/gradient-plot/.title: Gradient Plot Recipe
/recipes/gradient-plot/.description: cars is an array of plain row objects with one categorical field and one quantitative measure.

/recipes/annotations/.title: Annotation Recipe
/recipes/annotations/.description: films is an array of plain row objects containing the two position fields and the label field.

/recipes/path-ordering/.title: Path Ordering Recipe
/recipes/path-ordering/.description: observations is an array of plain row objects. Source rows may be shuffled; the order field controls vertices independently inside each color/group series.

/troubleshooting/.title: Troubleshooting
/troubleshooting/.description: ggaction reports an error when stored state does not determine one safe action. It does not silently select the first mark, scale, dataset, or coordinate.

/gallery/.title: Chart Gallery
/gallery/.description: Browse complete ggaction charts by Cartesian, statistical, Polar, composition, and interaction task.

/gallery/all/.title: All Chart Examples
/gallery/all/.description: Browse every maintained ggaction chart example by analytical task.

/api/.title: Chart API
/api/.description: Build a chart from a small set of domain actions. Start with the family that owns the decision you need to make; follow its focused pages only when the overview no longer provides…

/api/basic-charts/.title: Basic Charts
/api/basic-charts/.description: Basic chart actions create a complete mark, its encodings, and applicable guides in one traceable call.

/api/parallel-coordinates/.title: Parallel Coordinates
/api/parallel-coordinates/.description: Create and revise row-wise paths across ordered dimension-local scales and axes.

/api/canvas/.title: Canvas
/api/canvas/.description: Creates the program's single canvas and establishes bounds for later position encodings.

/api/data/.title: Data
/api/data/.description: Data actions own immutable source rows, explicit derivation provenance, and statistical result datasets.

/api/data/source-and-derived/.title: Source and Derived Data
/api/data/source-and-derived/.description: Empty arrays are valid, and row properties may contain nested arrays or objects. The action copies and freezes the supplied data.

/api/data/filtering/.title: Data Filtering
/api/data/filtering/.description: Create a named derived dataset without replacing or mutating its source.

/api/data/statistical-transforms/.title: Statistical Data Transforms
/api/data/statistical-transforms/.description: Create deterministic regression predictions from an existing dataset. This is an advanced data action used by higher level regression chart actions.

/api/data/window/.title: Window Data Transforms
/api/data/window/.description: createWindowData computes ordered values within optional partitions and stores the result as a new immutable dataset.

/api/data/bin2d/.title: Rectangular 2D Bins
/api/data/bin2d/.description: createBin2DData groups two quantitative fields into a rectangular grid. editBin2DData partially revises an existing logical grid owner.

/api/marks/.title: Marks
/api/marks/.description: Marks define the semantic form of a layer. Create a mark first, then connect it to data through position, grouping, and appearance encodings.

/api/marks/point/.title: Point Marks
/api/marks/point/.description: Point marks represent individual observations or derived items. Their semantic type is point; circle, square, diamond, and other symbols are graphical realizations of that meaning.

/api/marks/line-area/.title: Line and Area Marks
/api/marks/line-area/.description: Line and area marks materialize ordered backend neutral paths. Lines connect values; areas close two edges or one density edge against a baseline.

/api/marks/bar/.title: Bar Marks
/api/marks/bar/.description: Bar marks represent binned counts, aggregate categories, grouped or stacked partitions, and observed quantitative intervals.

/api/marks/rule/.title: Rule Marks
/api/marks/rule/.description: Rule marks represent reference lines and intervals. They use concrete line primitives without exposing a renderer specific path format.

/api/marks/text/.title: Text Marks
/api/marks/text/.description: Text marks turn data values into visible labels. Add one after a compatible point, bar, rect, or rule layer and ggaction persists that layer as the annotation source.

/api/marks/rect/.title: Rect Marks
/api/marks/rect/.description: Rect marks represent independent two dimensional cells. They are distinct from bars: rects do not infer aggregation, a zero baseline, stacking, or bar width.

/api/encodings/.title: Encodings
/api/encodings/.description: Encoding actions connect data fields or constants to chart channels. Ordinary authors choose the relationship; ggaction infers a unique target, coordinate, scale ID, and field…

/api/regression/.title: Regression
/api/regression/.description: createRegression() layers grouped linear, polynomial, or LOESS fits over an existing quantitative point mark.

/api/error-bars/.title: Error Bars
/api/error-bars/.description: createErrorBar() materializes vertical or horizontal intervals from either grouped statistics or existing center/lower/upper fields.

/api/error-bands/.title: Error Bands
/api/error-bands/.description: createErrorBand() creates a vertical or horizontal confidence or interval ribbon as one ordinary area layer.

/api/box-plots/.title: Box Plots
/api/box-plots/.description: createBoxPlot creates vertical or horizontal box plots from one categorical field and one quantitative field.

/api/gradient-plots/.title: Gradient Plots
/api/gradient-plots/.description: createGradientPlot compares a quantitative distribution across categories. Each category becomes one density filled strip plus an optional center rule.

/api/violin-plots/.title: Violin Plots
/api/violin-plots/.description: Use createViolinPlot to compare kernel density profiles inside categorical bands.

/api/guides/.title: Guides
/api/guides/.description: Creates the applicable Cartesian, Polar, or Parallel axes, Cartesian or Polar grid, and categorical legend supported by the current semantic encodings.

/api/titles/.title: Titles
/api/titles/.description: Creates one chart title and an optional subtitle on any Canvas edge.

/api/rendering/.title: Rendering
/api/rendering/.description: Render one fully materialized ChartProgram to Browser Canvas, a browser safe SVG string, a Node PNG file, or a single page vector PDF.

/api/composition/.title: Program Composition
/api/composition/.description: Combine already authored chart programs without merging their datasets, marks, scales, or guides.

/api/position-encodings/.title: Position Encodings
/api/position-encodings/.description: Choose the position family from the semantic mark and field relationship.

/api/position/quantitative/.title: Quantitative Positions
/api/position/quantitative/.description: Automatic domains combine compatible fields that share a scale. Automatic ranges use plot bounds, and every encoded value must be finite.

/api/position/temporal/.title: Temporal Line Positions
/api/position/temporal/.description: Parseable date strings and finite timestamps are normalized for scale resolution without changing the source dataset. The path remains empty until y is encoded.

/api/position/ordinal-bars/.title: Ordinal Bar Positions
/api/position/ordinal-bars/.description: Automatic domains preserve first appearance order. Automatic ranges use the horizontal plot bounds and resolve a shared step and bandwidth.

/api/position/histogram/.title: Histogram Positions
/api/position/histogram/.description: Prefer the atomic action for ordinary chart authoring:

/api/position/offsets/.title: Ordinal Offsets
/api/position/offsets/.description: That action calls encodeXOffset for a vertical bar or encodeYOffset for a horizontal bar as a wrapped child with the same field.

/api/series-encodings/.title: Series Encodings
/api/series-encodings/.description: Series appearance is authored through color, stroke dash, and stroke width families.

/api/series/color/.title: Color Encoding
/api/series/color/.description: Map a nominal or ordinal field to point fills, line series strokes, area fills, bar fills, rect fills, or arc sector fills.

/api/series/stroke-dash/.title: Stroke Dash Encoding
/api/series/stroke-dash/.description: Map a nominal field to line series or rule dash patterns, or apply one constant pattern to every series.

/api/appearance/.title: Appearance Encodings
/api/appearance/.description: Appearance actions change final mark style without changing position. Use the focused pages below for selection, point appearance, and mark specific style.

/api/appearance/selection-and-highlighti.title: Selection and Highlighting
/api/appearance/selection-and-highlighti.description: selectMarks is the advanced reusable selection action. Selection by itself does not alter semanticSpec or graphicSpec:

/api/appearance/point/.title: Point Appearance
/api/appearance/point/.description: Broadcast a non negative finite graphical radius to a point mark.

/api/appearance/mark-style/.title: Mark Style
/api/appearance/mark-style/.description: encodeStroke({ value, target? }) assigns a required non empty constant color string to a rule.

/api/scales/.title: Scale Options
/api/scales/.description: Scales map semantic field or datum values into concrete positions and appearance values.

/api/scales/position/.title: Position Scales
/api/scales/position/.description: The field type, channel, mark recipe, and all consumers of a shared scale must agree. editScale validates that complete matrix before applying any semantic or graphical change.

/api/scales/ordinal-and-dash/.title: Ordinal and Dash Scales
/api/scales/ordinal-and-dash/.description: Band/point position, color, and stroke dash encodings use ordered discrete domains. Automatic domains preserve first appearance order.

/api/scales/continuous-color/.title: Continuous Color Scales
/api/scales/continuous-color/.description: Use a name directly or an object with optional sampling controls:

/api/scales/discretized-color/.title: Discretized Color Scales
/api/scales/discretized-color/.description: Quantitative point color can create concrete color classes instead of a continuous gradient:

/api/scales/missing-values/.title: Missing Scale Values
/api/scales/missing-values/.description: Point encodings can provide an unknown fallback inside their scale options:

/api/coordinates/.title: Coordinates
/api/coordinates/.description: Position encoding actions normally manage coordinates automatically:

/api/axes/.title: Axes
/api/axes/.description: Creates complete axes for encoded Cartesian x/y, Polar theta/radius, or Parallel dimension channels. This is the recommended axis action for ordinary chart authoring.

/api/grids/.title: Grids
/api/grids/.description: Creates Cartesian or Polar grid geometry from encoded scales. Cartesian defaults to horizontal only.

/api/legends/.title: Legends
/api/legends/.description: Legends are inferred from final mark encodings and materialized as concrete graphics.

/api/legends/categorical/.title: Categorical and Size Legends
/api/legends/categorical/.description: Creates inferred legend blocks. It supports combined line series, color stacked histogram, grouped ordinal bar, grouped area, composite point series, quantitative point size,…

/api/legends/continuous/.title: Continuous Legends
/api/legends/continuous/.description: A point color encoding with a quantitative or temporal field produces a continuous gradient. Right/left positions orient it vertically; top/bottom orient it horizontally.

/api/legends/composite/.title: Composite Legend Symbols
/api/legends/composite/.description: Legend symbols are graphical recipes composed from line, point, and swatch layers. The default line shorthand remains supported:

/api/legends/editing/.title: Editing Legends
/api/legends/editing/.description: editLegend() updates one existing stable legend. Omit target when exactly one legend target exists; otherwise pass its mark ID.

/concepts/chart-program/.title: ChartProgram and Immutability
/concepts/chart-program/.description: chart() returns an empty ChartProgram. A program contains the authored chart state and the history used to produce it.

/concepts/semantic-and-graphics/.title: Semantic and Graphical State
/concepts/semantic-and-graphics/.description: ggaction keeps chart meaning separate from its fully resolved graphics.

/concepts/actions-and-trace/.title: Actions and Trace Trees
/concepts/actions-and-trace/.description: Every chart authoring method is an action. Calling a high level action records that action and any wrapped actions it invokes as children.

/advanced/axis-components/.title: Advanced Axis Components
/advanced/axis-components/.description: Use these actions when a complete createAxes call is not sufficient. All component actions require a resolved linear, time, or ordinal scale and Canvas bounds.

/supported-features/.title: Supported Features
/supported-features/.description: This page describes implemented behavior only. A dash means that the current chart authoring API does not support that combination.

/reference/actions/.title: Complete Action Reference
/reference/actions/.description: Find every public ggaction action by task, API layer, or exact action name.

/reference/actions/charts-data/.title: Charts, Data, and Composition Actions
/reference/actions/charts-data/.description: Create complete charts, manage data, select marks, and compose complete programs.

/reference/actions/marks/.title: Mark Actions
/reference/actions/marks/.description: Create, edit, jitter, and remove semantic chart marks.

/reference/actions/encodings/.title: Encoding Actions
/reference/actions/encodings/.description: Map fields and constants to position, grouping, color, shape, size, and appearance.

/reference/actions/statistics/.title: Statistical Layer Actions
/reference/actions/statistics/.description: Create and edit regression, density, interval, error, and box-plot layers.

/reference/actions/guides/.title: Guide, Axis, Grid, and Title Actions
/reference/actions/guides/.description: Create, edit, and remove axes, grids, legends, and chart titles.

/reference/actions/advanced/.title: Advanced Chart Actions
/reference/actions/advanced/.description: Author explicit semantic resources and control individual Cartesian axis and grid components.

/reference/actions/extension/.title: Extension Actions
/reference/actions/extension/.description: Use wrapped actions and low-level semantic, graphic, and scale primitives to extend ggaction.

/reference/runtime/.title: Program and Rendering Functions
/reference/runtime/.description: Create, compose, render, and inspect programs without confusing package functions with chainable actions.

/reference/types/.title: Exact TypeScript Contract
/reference/types/.description: Read the complete generated TypeScript interface for every chainable ChartProgram action.

/extension/action-authoring/.title: Action Authoring
/extension/action-authoring/.description: The extension entry point is for developers adding traceable domain actions.

/extension/primitives/.title: Primitive API
/extension/primitives/.description: These methods are the low level public extension layer, not the recommended chart authoring API.
